channel.title: Blogging on Logs from the Matrix
channel.description: Recent content in Blogging on Logs from the Matrix

item.title: Why I Chose Hugo (And Ditched Jekyll)
item.author: michael@quinton.io (mikequinton)
item.description: When creating a blog, you’ve got a ton of options. Some are just not as simple as Hugo. Hugo has its downsides, sure—but for me, the pros easily outweigh the cons. I tried Jekyll at first. The problem? Ruby dependencies. It got annoying fast. I didn’t feel like debugging stuff before I even got to the hard part: actually writing. Weird thing to say as someone in tech, maybe… but I’d rather save the troubleshooting for when it’s really needed.

item.title: Starting Again
item.author: michael@quinton.io (mikequinton)
item.description: For me, I’m the type of person who says I’m starting something and means it… but actually seeing it through? That part tends to fall off. I started a blog in the past, posted maybe once or twice, and that was that. Nothing really came of it. But recently, I’ve been inspired again. A friend of mine, Chris, has been consistently posting content—mostly about Kubernetes and tech stuff he finds interesting.
